item: P0.4 unlock-at-scale root-cause + fix: ensure_fts rebuilds the FTS index only when needed
status: fixed on synthetic, awaiting live validation (draft PR onto 0.2)
summary: ROOT CAUSE of the recurring 981s->1645s field unlock: init_db called ensure_fts which ran the external-content FTS5 'rebuild' UNCONDITIONALLY on EVERY unlock, re-reading every article's title+content through the SQLCipher codec (corpus-scaled, recurring -- an index build would be one-time). The article_fts_ai/ad/au triggers already keep the index current, so the rebuild is redundant after the first build. FIX: ensure_fts(rebuild='auto') rebuilds ONLY when needed (fresh table with existing articles, or a present-but-EMPTY index probed via the article_fts_docsize shadow table since SELECT..FROM article_fts reads the CONTENT table on an external-content index); steady-state boots SKIP it. MEASURED on a 112k-article/2.7GB ENCRYPTED synthetic corpus (GAMMA harness): OLD per-boot rebuild 28.6s -> NEW auto skip 0.002s (18924x); full G2 unlock_bench warm (steady-state) unlock 0.012s (acceptance <2s). Skeptic (negative-space) found the docsize LIMIT-1 probe self-heals only a FULLY-empty index not a PARTIAL one; MEASURED the suggested count-comparison hardening and REJECTED it (count(*) FROM articles 74s cold = worse than the rebuild) -- a partial/corrupt index is unreachable via normal operation (atomic ingest+trigger; atomic rebuild; restore rebuilds in verify_copy) and is the fts_status(D4)+re-index job's domain; docstring states it honestly. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 8 rebuild-gate + 72 FTS/search/scale tests green, full suite 3361 passed, ruff F/B, mypy 127=baseline. 3 pre-push skeptics completed (A1/A7/A8).

item: Bump the corpus epoch after a committed restore-merge (DB-7)
summary: The corpus epoch forces the disposable derived rollups (keyword_daily/source_coverage serves) to FULL-rebuild after a non-append mutation; it was wired into re-index/prune but NOT the restore-merge -- 'the one residual mutator' (P1.6/A7). The post-swap re-index of imported articles DID bump, but only when reindex_imported=True + non-empty import set + the re-index succeeded; reindex_imported=False (the merge-engine/torture path), an empty import, or a re-index hiccup after the additive commit all skipped it. run_restore now bumps once, unconditionally, right after the atomic swap (over-bumping is harmless). Best-effort so a coordination write never undoes a committed restore. Skeptic verified: session_scope binds to the RESTORED live db, a bump failure can't undo the commit, the double-bump-with-reindex is harmless. VERIFIED: test_restore_bumps_epoch + 23 merge/restore tests green.

item: Align the alembic stamp to head after schema self-heal (DB-8)
summary: The boot self-heals bring an old store's SCHEMA to head without moving the alembic stamp = a 'lying stamp' (behind head, schema ahead) that breaks the next real migration and the cross-version restore's alembic upgrade (re-adding already-present columns -> 'duplicate column', confirmed at prepare_staged_corpus). align_stamp_to_head advances the stamp to head ONLY when (1) compare_metadata (FTS5-shadow-filtered via an EXACT set, not startswith) verifies the schema fully at head AND (2) the stamp is at/after a DATA FLOOR (a5b6c7d8e9f0 = child of the newest fabricated/wrong-data migration f4b5c6d7e8a9 null-reliability-score / a3b4c5d6e7f8 normalize-country) via a robust ancestry check -- so a store genuinely behind (missing column) OR that would keep fabricated/wrong data keeps its stamp and migrates instead. Wired into init_db after the self-heals; cheap stamp-check runs before the metadata compare (compare_metadata only paid on the transient behind-stamp store, never steady state). Skeptic verified no wrong-advance produces a missing table/column/index/constraint (compare_metadata catches all schema diffs; the data floor closes the pure-data-migration honesty gap the skeptic found). VERIFIED: 8 stamp-align tests + migration-drift green.

item: Standalone verify for the folder backup (wiki dumps / OSM / models)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.2; UI wiring -> Session B)
summary: The volumes backup has /v2/volumes/verify; the folder backup (the large-data copy) had none, so a field-corrupted external-drive backup couldn't be checked before a restore relied on it. verify_folder_backup checks a folder backup against its manifest HONESTLY to what it stores: every listed file present with the exact recorded size, and the content-addressed Ollama model blobs (blobs/sha256-<hex>) additionally content-hashed against their name (bounded RAM, streamed); wiki dumps + OSM extracts carry NO stored checksum (immutable public re-downloads) so they are size-verified only (size_only) -- never dressed up as a content check. Manifest name->path fields are traversal-guarded before any stat/hash (an external-drive manifest is untrusted). Runs as a new 'verify' mode on FolderBackupManager (visible/cancellable in /api/jobs; verdict rides status()['verify'], schema oo-folder-verify-1). Endpoint POST /api/backup/folder/verify. Skeptic (negative-space) found 2 real defects, FIXED: a non-dict 'categories' raised AttributeError (broke 'never raises'), and a corrupted-but-parseable manifest silently read ok=True; both now honest ok=False with malformed_entries counted; traversal + checksum verified solid. VERIFIED: 11 folder-verify tests + folder-backup regression green.

item: Opt-in persistent data_dir + honest ephemeral-root note (DispVM durability)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.2; UI note wiring -> Session B)
key_paths: src/monitoring/forensics.py src/api/main.py src/api/diagnostics.py scripts/launch.sh install.sh tests/test_data_dir_persistence.py
summary: The 2026-07-09 field event: a disposable-VM crash vaporized a ~60K-article corpus. Two halves, both HONEST (never 'stop using disposable VMs' -- only how to keep the corpus): (1) EASY OPT-IN persistence -- the installer now records OO_DATA_DIR (OO_DATA_DIR=/mnt/persist/oo ./install.sh validates creatable+writable and writes it 0600 into oo.env, which launch.sh sources every start) so a bind-mounted/external volume survives across restarts. (2) HONEST DETECTION -- data_dir_persistence() signals only what it can PROVE: a RAM-backed (tmpfs) data folder is definitely volatile; a Qubes disposable VM is provable via qubesdb; everything else is 'unknown' (never a guess). When at_risk it emits a one-time boot warning + a note pointing at OO_DATA_DIR, surfaced via GET /api/diagnostics/data-dir-persistence and embedded in session_forensics. VERIFIED: 8 persistence tests green, bash -n both scripts, bandit -ll clean.

item: B2: regression guard pinning the FRED 2-letter ISO convention for OECD share-price index ids
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.2; test-only; the config fix itself landed 2026-07-09)
summary: Field-test 2026-07-08 Item 1: the OECD SPASTT01<CC>M661N feed ids had used a 3-letter ISO code (SPASTT01DEUM661N) that does not exist on FRED, so every OECD index 404'd and Europe/Asia(ex-Nikkei)/Africa/S.America/Oceania boards showed empty. The config fix (ISO-3->ISO-2) already landed on 0.2 (2026-07-09), but no test pinned the convention. item: B1: offline zh/ja/th word segmentation via the [segmentation] extra (jieba/janome/pythainlp) + ko/mr stoplists (2a ruling executed)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.2; py3.13 venv green; CI exercises it via the added [segmentation] extra; Core-only job proves graceful degrade)
key_paths: src/analytics/segmentation.py src/analytics/extract.py src/analytics/managed.py src/analytics/engine_report.py src/analytics/selftest.py src/services/stopwords.py scripts/build_stopwords.py configs/stopwords_iso/{zh,ja,th,ko,mr}.txt configs/external_artifacts.yml pyproject.toml .github/workflows/ci.yml tests/test_segmentation.py
summary: Maintainer delegated 2a: pick+ship license-clean offline zh/ja/th segmenters. CHOSEN: jieba (MIT, zh) + janome (Apache-2.0 bundled dict, ja) + pythainlp newmm (Apache-2.0, th) -- all pure-local, tokenize over dictionaries bundled IN THE WHEEL (NO model download, NO network; verified offline), pip-installable via a new [segmentation] extra (no repo vendoring -> no 100MB-rule/license duplication; no *_AS_OF so no registry entry needed per the freshness protocol). SEAM: new src/analytics/segmentation.py exposes segment(text,lang)->[(word,offset)]|None (jieba yields offsets directly; janome/pythainlp reconstructed via forward-cursor find, exact since surfaces concatenate to input) + segmenter_available(lang) (lightweight __import__ check, does NOT load dicts). Hooked into extract._terms(): when segment() returns tokens use them with a language-aware min_len=2 (CJK words are 2 chars: 中国/政策/経済 -- the Latin 3-char floor would drop them), else the byte-identical whitespace _WORD_RE path (min_len=_MIN_TERM_LEN=3). GRACEFUL DEGRADE by construction: extra absent OR OO_SEGMENTATION=0 -> segment()=None -> old whitespace tokenizer, zh/ja/th stay honestly 'unsegmented' (a core install is byte-unchanged; proven by test + the Core-only CI job). STATUS FLIP: managed.language_status() is segmenter-aware (zh/ja/th -> 'functional' only when a segmenter is present + the vendored stoplist applies); is_managed refactored to language_status==functional (byte-identical for existing langs); engine_report._lang_status routes through the ONE source of truth; ko(Hangul)+mr(Marathi) added to MANAGED_LANGUAGES (space-segmented, distinct scripts, now carry stopwords-iso lists). STOPLISTS vendored from stopwords-iso 0.7.1 (byte-identical to 0.7.0 for existing langs; sr/az stay honestly uncovered -- absent from stopwords-iso): zh 794 / ja 134 / th 116 / ko 679 / mr 99; STOPWORDS_ISO_AS_OF 2026-06->2026-07 + registry last_verified 2026-07-10 (external-freshness green). MEASURED junk reduction on fixtures: OFF the whitespace tokenizer minted whole SENTENCES as one keyword (中国政府今天宣布了新的经济政策 = one 'keyword') / Thai mark-fragments; ON it yields real REPEATING words (经济/政策/市场/影响, 経済/政府/市場, เศรษฐกิจ/รัฐบาล/ผลกระทบ). The corpus-level win is that real words RECUR across articles (Heaps beta drops from ~0.95), making aggregations meaningful. 3 selftest golden cases (segmentation_{zh,ja,th}, gated on segmenter_available like the lemma case) + tests/test_segmentation.py (graceful-degrade tests run everywhere; segmenter-present tests skip without the extra; adversarial empty/whitespace/emoji/mixed-script/4000-char inputs never crash, offsets validated). NEGATIVE-SPACE verified (skeptic pass): byte-identical fallback, offset fidelity, no import cycle/heavy status-check load, kill switch, mixed-script drops digits keeps Latin+CJK. RETROACTIVE PATH: a keyword-only re-index applies segmentation to an existing corpus. VERIFIED py3.13 venv: 55 seg/managed/report + 241 keyword/freshness/invariants green; ruff F/B clean; mypy 0 new in-file errors; selftest 46/46.

item: B4: hu/fa relative-day words (measure-first: the field-test fa 0%/hu 22% figures were STALE)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.2; py3.13 venv green; datediag lockstep auto-mirrored)
summary: MEASURE-FIRST (per the brief): probed the CURRENT tree before building and found the field-test 2026-07-08 'fa 0% / hu 22%' figures STALE -- fa Jalali (15 اسفند 1402 -> 2024-03-05) + Persian numerals + Gregorian transliterations, and hu named (2024. marcius 15-en) + numeric (2024.03.15) + historic (1848) dates ALL already extract correctly (the #590 Jalali claim-on-route work moved fa; the batch-A/wave-8 work moved hu). The genuine residual was RELATIVE DAY words, which fa/hu were the only major languages missing. Added ONLY the collision-free forms with the negative-space lens: hu tegnap(-1)/holnap(1); fa دیروز(-1, yesterday). item: B5: backup UX truth-telling (job-state-as-truth poll+start, paused-state label, verify/pause-resume wiring)
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.2; frontend browser-unverified; skeptic-reviewed, 3 defects fixed; 13 UI tests + i18n 100% green)
summary: Field-test Item 9 (data-safety UX). (a) _uxPoll no longer prints a fatal 'Backup failed: NetworkError' on a dropped /volumes/status poll while the job runs fine: transport hiccups retry with capped backoff (MAX_FAILS=40, 15s cap, ~9min ceiling) showing an honest 'connection hiccup - retrying'; ONLY a backend-reported error/cancelled STATE is a real failure (job-state-as-truth). (a2) _uxStartThenPoll extends the same truth to the START/RESUME/VERIFY request: the start POST returns AFTER the worker thread spawns, so a lost RESPONSE (job running server-side) must not read as failure - on a start error it consults /status and only running|paused proves the job is live (falls through to the poll); NOT 'done' (a just-started job can't be instantly done, so a stale 'done' from a prior run never masks a failed start as complete); a genuine 400/409 (or an unreachable /status) still re-throws and surfaces. (b) Paused != complete (the paused-state label): the export dialog gains a Pause/Resume control (i18n-robust via dataset.mode, not translated-text comparison); a paused job shows 'Backup paused. Resume to continue where it left off.' instead of 'Backup complete'; resume continues from the volume resume-log / already-copied files (never re-does finished work), the folder phase uses its dedicated /folder/resume and re-enables the Pause button for the resumed multi-GB copy. (c) The shipped verify job (/api/backup/v2/volumes/verify) is wired into the Import dialog: verify a backup's manifest signature + every volume + parity checksum WITHOUT restoring (the live corpus untouched; optional passphrase = deep decrypt-check into a hash sink), rendering the honest report (bad/missing volumes, parity tolerance, recoverable-from-parity) - counts only, no score; the verify phase label is mode-aware translated (never a raw 'verifying'). SKEPTIC PASS (general-purpose agent, adversarial, 271k tokens): found 3 REAL defects - MED folder-resume left Pause stuck-disabled through the resume, MED-LOW the start/resume/verify requests were still bare (Item-9 symptom moved to the start path), LOW verify's mode selected a phase map with no 'verifying' key -> raw untranslated label - all 3 fixed + pinned. Confirmed-clean: fails-reset, bounded backoff, error/cancelled surface s.error, verify body {src,passphrase:''} matches VolumeVerifyBody, report read at s.summary.report, all backend strings esc()'d. VERIFIED: node --check, 13 test_unified_backup_ui tests, i18n --min 100 (1976x12). New strings English-fallback via t() (keyable in the B10 tail). The Database section's 'on disk X' now also appends the all-stores total once measured (reads the cache, no extra walk), so the DB number never implies it is the whole app's disk use. HONESTY refinement: the per-component tag is 'private (local)' not 'encrypted' (SQLCipher-encryption is strictly true only of the DB triple; staging/other need not be), while the summary keeps the brief's 'encrypted private corpus' framing where the DB dominates the private bytes. Backend contract verified exactly (smoke-ran storage_footprint(): kinds db/wal/shm/wiki_dumps/osm_regions/staging/other/ollama_models match _SF_PUBLIC; totals.grand_total_bytes present). item: B15: opt-in local-LLM language detection as a THIRD labelled provenance class
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.2; backend py3.13 green [ruff/mypy/10 tests]; frontend browser-unverified; negative-space skeptic-reviewed)
key_paths: src/ai_layer/langdetect_llm.py src/ai_layer/translate.py src/api/ai.py src/static/app.js src/static/index.html tests/test_ai_langdetect.py tests/test_repo_invariants.py
summary: Maintainer 2026-07-10 addendum B15. Articles with no language tag that the fast offline py3langid detector could not classify (short text / low confidence / an unsupported language) stay stuck in the '?' bucket; this OPT-IN pass asks the local model to identify them and records the answer as a THIRD, clearly-labelled 'AI-derived - unreliable' provenance class (ai_keyword kind='language'), shown inline in the article view (the existing convention). BINDING CONSTRAINTS, all honesty-by-construction: (1) DETECTOR-FIRST - unknown_language_work() selects only articles with BOTH Article.language AND detected_language unset (or_(is None, == '')), so the cheap deterministic offline pass always runs first and a tagged article is never re-processed. (2) NEVER OVERWRITES the trusted channels - writes ONLY via ai_store.record_keywords to ai_keyword; Article.language/detected_language are untouched (proven behaviourally + a structural invariant that the module has no dotted .language assignment). (3) GARBAGE STORES NOTHING - parse_lang requires the reply to CLEAN to a code in the app's KNOWN_LANG_CODES vocab (translate._LANG_NAMES keys, +ko/he/mr/ta/hr/et/lt/lv/az): after stripping a leading 'Language:'-style label + surrounding quotes/punct the whole reply must BE a known code, NOT contain one - a chatty sentence ('It is French') is rejected because English 'it'(Italian)/'no'(Norwegian)/'he'(Hebrew) are themselves codes, so scanning-inside-prose would mislabel (the #590 negative-space lesson); refusals/'unknown'/'mixed' rejected; a made-up code rejected. (4) OPT-IN + VISIBLE + ABORTABLE - a user-started, cancellable, task-manager-visible BackgroundJob (kind='ai-langdetect', is_writer, auto-enumerated in /api/jobs via all_job_statuses; should_stop=ctx.stopping), NEVER on the scrape hot path; local loopback only; airplane mode / Ollama-down makes is_available() false -> a clean no-op (never a wall of failed events); commit-per-article (short write-gate window). Endpoints POST /api/ai/detect-language{,/cancel} + GET .../status + .../candidates (the worklist size so the UI shows if there's anything to do). Frontend: a Settings -> AI opt-in panel + button (runLangDetect) with job-state-as-truth progress polling (a dropped poll = 'connection hiccup - retrying', never 'failed') + the candidate count on subtab open. tests/test_ai_langdetect.py (9, ISOLATED in-memory engine per the #577 pollution lesson: parse accept/reject incl. the chatty/ambiguous negative space, detector-first worklist, stores-only-ai_keyword-never-Article.language, garbage->nothing, skip-existing top-up, cancel-stops-early, outage-aborts-loudly) + test_repo_invariants::test_llm_langdetect_is_optin_labelled_and_never_touches_trusted_channels. VERIFIED py3.13: ruff clean (project config), mypy 0-new in my files, 10 targeted + 186 sweep green (the lone sweep failure is the pre-existing stale B6 test fixed in #625, absent from this branch). REMAINING: human click-through (fork-3); a broader ISO set if the maintainer wants to label genuinely-unsupported languages (currently the KNOWN vocab = the app's usable + common-corpus languages).

item: canonical-basename LOAD — fixes the DuckDB init-symbol mangling that reddened the columnar CI lane; skeptic-hardened
status: shipped (draft PR onto 0.2; skeptic-verified; real-httpfs round-trip is the CI-only proof)
summary: The columnar CI-red root cause: DuckDB derives an extension's C init symbol <name>_init from the LOADed file BASENAME split on the FIRST dot (FileSystem::ExtractBaseName), so the version-dotted bundled name httpfs-<plat>-v1.5.4.duckdb_extension derived the bogus httpfs-<plat>-v1 -> a nonexistent httpfs-<plat>-v1_init symbol -> LOAD failed -> secure_crypto_available() False -> the persisted-encrypted store silently fell back to in-memory, reddening the 'Columnar store' lane's real-httpfs round-trip (test_ci_encrypted_persisted_round_trip). FIX: new _canonical_httpfs_path() copies the already-SHA-verified bytes into a per-process temp dir under the canonical basename httpfs.duckdb_extension (so DuckDB derives httpfs -> httpfs_init); _persisted_connection() LOADs that instead of the dotted path. Trust unchanged: the SHA-256 pin + DuckDB version coupling + traversal guard still run on the REAL bundled file (refactored _verified_httpfs returning (path,sha)) BEFORE the copy. SKEPTIC-HARDENED (adversarial negative-space pass, MED finding fixed): the cache is keyed on the verified DIGEST (not the source path, so a re-pin to different bytes at the same path invalidates) AND the cached copy is re-hashed against that digest before every reuse (so an in-place tamper/corruption of the temp copy is caught and the stale copy is never served) -> verify-before-LOAD covers the actually-LOADed artifact on EVERY call, not just the source proxy; thread-locked; temp dir removed on invalidation + at interpreter exit (single atexit); blank pins stay a pure no-op (zero temp copies). Regression tests (pure-Python, no real binary): a canonical-basename LOAD derives 'httpfs' while the version-dotted name mangles, _persisted_connection hands the canonical path to LOAD not the dotted one, unbundled -> no copy, and the tampered-copy/re-pin cases re-copy the verified bytes. Real-httpfs round-trip is CI-only (extensions.duckdb.org egress-blocked in-sandbox) = the 'Columnar store' lane is the confirmation. Does NOT itself unblock production D1/D2/D3: the registry pins still ship BLANK, so the operator must bundle+pin the per-OS binaries; the fix removes only the symbol-mangling blocker so secure_crypto_available() returns True once a verified binary is present (proven by the CI lane). ruff F/B + mypy clean on the changed file; 23 loader/engine tests pass locally (2 CI-only skipped). README duckdb_ext updated with the canonical-basename note.

THE LOAD-BEARING REFRAME: audits whether a source's scrapes are usable ARTICLES (vs nav/stub/paywall/wrong-DOM pages), NEVER editorial merit -- terse/atypical prose is legitimate variety and can never reach degraded/failing for it; only the pathology (furniture-repetition / nav-DOM) extraction-failure signature, corroborated, can (soft style-ambiguous signals never exceed 'watch'). area: backup — large-data export silently skipped the corpus (Slice 0, DATA-SAFETY)

status: shipped (draft PR #670 onto 0.2; source-guard + backend-contract tests; BROWSER-UNVERIFIED per fork-3)
summary: FIX_SESSION_2026-07-14 Slice 0 (top priority). Regression from B5 2a10cd3: all volume ops share ONE VolumeBackupManager + ONE /v2/volumes/status; _uxStartThenPoll masked a lost/409 start by adopting ANY live job (running|paused) without checking mode/dest, and _uxRun treated its 'done' as this backup's completion -> ran folder/start (blobs) + printed success, corpus for THIS dest never written (repro: a concurrent Verify or a backup to another drive is live). FIX restores the invariant 'the blob phase is unreachable + Backup-complete never shown unless the volumes phase PROVABLY completed as a backup of the corpus into THIS dest': _uxRun aborts (into the existing catch -> Backup failed) unless s1.state===done && s1.mode===backup && _uxSamePath(dest) (a false abort is a SAFE retry; a false success is silent data loss -- biased to abort); _uxStartThenPoll takes expect={mode,dest} and re-throws a masked start whose live job isn't ours. Backend NOT at fault (stream_backup.py:928 emits the corpus member unconditionally). Source-level guards pin the _uxRun gate + the _uxStartThenPoll re-throw + the corpus-member contract; node --check clean; i18n 100% (new string English-fallback via t()). area: database — is_locked_error/retry dead on encrypted stores (Slice 1, data-integrity)

status: shipped (#669 MERGED + follow-up draft PR #671; negative-space lens pinned)
summary: FIX_SESSION_2026-07-14 Slice 1. is_locked_error now matches the locked/busy MESSAGE on the DB-layer OperationalError classes (SQLAlchemy's + stdlib sqlite3 + sqlcipher3, cached) across the __cause__/__context__ chain -- narrow BY CONSTRUCTION: the message is only honoured on an actual DB OperationalError class, so an IntegrityError (or any non-OperationalError mentioning the words) is NEVER treated as locked and still takes the dedup/redo path. The batch.py 297-loss path (broad except Exception + is_locked_error) is re-armed by this. FOLLOW-UP #671: run_write_with_retry caught except OperationalError (SQLAlchemy's) narrowly, so a RAW sqlcipher3 lock escaped its retry entirely, unprotecting the markets/import/store writers (the 2026-06-13 commodities-loss class on encrypted stores) -- broadened to except Exception with is_locked_error as the discriminator (non-locks re-raise immediately, behaviour unchanged). NEGATIVE-SPACE pinned: non-lock OperationalError/IntegrityError/bare-message-no-type are NOT locked; a raw sqlite3 lock + a wrapped cause + (CI) a real sqlcipher3 lock ARE; run_write_with_retry retries a raw lock to success + re-raises a non-lock on the first attempt. ruff/mypy clean.

item: auto-install the missing venv apt package instead of dying with manual steps (maintainer field report: installing on Tails)
status: shipped + MERGED (#677 onto 0.2; 29 installer tests green; skeptic-reviewed)
summary: On Tails/minimal-Debian `python3 -m venv` fails because the stdlib venv/ensurepip ships in a SEPARATE apt package that isn't preinstalled. create_venv now installs it AUTOMATICALLY via a new try_apt_install_venv (elevate via sudo or run as root) instead of the old die()-with-instructions. HONEST + SAFE by construction: NEVER hangs on an unanswerable sudo prompt (probes passwordless `sudo -n` first; a password prompt is only allowed in an interactive, non---unattended/--appvm session with a TTY -- both --appvm and --unattended set UNATTENDED=1, so CI/scripted flows only auto-install on passwordless sudo); REFUSES to claim success unless ensurepip is importable afterwards (the apt-ran-but-still-missing case falls back, never a false 'installed'); OO_NO_APT=1 opts out; command -v apt-get absent (macOS) falls back; on any failure falls back to a Tails-aware die() (admin password set at the Welcome Screen, apt over Tor, amnesia -> Persistent Storage/Additional Software). KNOWN LIMITATION stated in QUICKSTART + the fallback: Tails is Debian 12 = Python 3.11, so a 3.13 interpreter must already be present (OO_PYTHON) and 3.13/python3.13-venv are NOT in Tails' default repos -- the fix closes the venv-PACKAGE gap, not the interpreter gap (never fabricated a Tails claim; every fact was web-verified). Tests: extract-the-function bash harness (opt-out / no-hang / seamless-success / honest-failure-when-ensurepip-still-missing / root) + a source-wiring+Tails-honesty grep; all 5 branches + 29/29 installer tests verified locally.

item: read-only DB + per-member guard + wall-clock budget (a slow/failing member never aborts or stalls the bundle)
summary: The bundle opened the DB read-write and computed most members inline. Now: read_only_db (query_only WAL snapshot); EVERY member individually guarded via _member (a raise records {error} even with a broken exception __str__); NON-DB members run on a daemon thread with a per-member wall-clock budget (OO_DEBUG_BUNDLE_MEMBER_BUDGET_S, default 20 s, clamped finite/<=3600) recording {skipped: budget}; DB members run INLINE (a shared SQLite connection is unsafe on a worker thread), bounded by a statement deadline. The first cut (threaded DB members + a deadline margin) drew a NO-GO from two skeptics -- statement_deadline bounds only SQL opcodes not the Python around them, concurrent pysqlite use BLOCKS not errors, SQLAlchemy Session is not thread-safe, self-deadlining members' inner finally clears the outer handler, a leaked handler contaminates the reused all-diagnostics session -- so DB members were removed from worker threads entirely (all four findings die by construction). A negative-space skeptic also found budget=inf crashed the whole bundle via Thread.join(inf) OverflowError (clamp fix) + a broken __str__ silently lost a member (_err_str fix). A re-verification skeptic on the hybrid returned GO. Two source-pin tests updated in lockstep.

area: fix-forward — WWW-pass savepoint vs datestore internal commit (#691 regression)
item: CI red on main since #691: test_wherewho_ingest places==0; root-caused + fixed forward on the docs-review PR branch
summary: #691 isolated index_article's when/where/who pass in session.begin_nested(), but datestore.store_for_article ends with an internal db.commit() — inside the caller's savepoint that commit closes the nested-transaction context, so the very next statement (whostore places delete) raises ''Can't operate on closed transaction inside context manager'', swallowed by the pass's by-design except => every article WITH a newly-extracted date silently stored NO places/entities (field regression since 2026-07-15 evening; a re-index restores the rows). Reproduced standalone (the swallow path's own py3.11 import failure surfaced the real exception), fixed savepoint-aware: in_nested_transaction() -> flush (rows join the caller's savepoint; caller commits), else commit (the /api/dates endpoint + index_recent keep exact semantics). Regression test test_store_inside_caller_savepoint_keeps_transaction_usable (pure ORM, runs on py3.11); repro re-run: places/entities/dates all >=1. ruff F,B clean; per-file mypy 0 in changed file; test_wherewho_ingest + the #691 poisoning test are CI-gated here (PEP-695 in write.py blocks py3.11). Lesson recorded (store helpers must never own the commit under a caller savepoint).

area: diagnostics — page-size A/B bench (DB-10 §1b measurement instrument)
item: maintainer-asked ''add a diagnostic tool to test that idea'' — run on a small + a large corpus, compare the logs; the TREND is the page_size decision signal
status: shipped (rides PR #698 onto main; panel browser-unverified per fork-3)
summary: run_pagesize_ab rebuilds the live corpus per candidate page size (plaintext VACUUM INTO; encrypted sqlcipher_export into an ATTACHed target keyed with the SAME passphrase so the SQLCipher codec stays IN the measurement) with auto_vacuum=INCREMENTAL (the ruled 1a default), SELF-VERIFIES every rebuilt target (pragmas read back + article count; BenchVerifyError refuses a mismatch — the verify-before-build probe made permanent so the pragma inheritance is proven on every run, never asserted), then runs the identical deterministic workload per size (point lookups by id / a 30-day covering-index window over keyword_mentions / bounded sequential content bands; first-pass cold-ish vs second-pass warm, p50/p95 + n) — numbers SIDE BY SIDE, never a composite/winner; rebuild.seconds doubles as the measured DB-10 store-migration cost at that corpus size. Sequential staging (one rebuild at a time, deleted after) under a swept .pagesize-bench- prefix (the P0.2 janitor lesson) + free-disk preflight (refuses honestly; optional external work_dir). Wired as BackgroundJob pagesize-bench (is_writer=False, cancellable between phases, cancel cleans stages + marks the partial report cancelled) with the full p0-validation-style surface (start/status/cancel/last/download) + a Diagnostics panel block; the last report rides the all-diagnostics bundle. EMPIRICAL: VACUUM INTO inherits page_size+auto_vacuum from the source connection (probed in-sandbox, pinned as a test). 8 tests (rebuild honors+self-verifies / refuses bad size / refuses a lying target / side-by-side shape + banned-key walk + stage cleanup / disk refusal / cancel-between-phases / stale-stage sweep / last-report stub+roundtrip) + the composed-route wiring guard; node --check + per-file mypy 0 + project-config ruff clean.
